# loam.dev

> loam.dev is a Dart CLI (command: `loam`) for codebase intelligence and anti-AI-slop on Dart and Flutter projects. It surfaces unused public API, circular dependencies and complexity hotspots as Findings, then holds them behind a Baseline so the CI Gate fails only on new regressions (Ratchet mode). It is built on the Dart `analyzer` package — semantic, element-level analysis rather than regex heuristics. Runs locally and in CI; deterministic and offline by default.

The product name is always `loam.dev`; `loam` is the CLI command and the pub.dev package name. The canonical web domain is `getloam.dev`.

## Core concepts

- A **Rule** is one analysis unit (unused public API, circular dependencies, complexity hotspots, slop). Each capability is a Rule behind one interface.
- A **Finding** is a single result: `{ ruleId, severity, location, message, fingerprint }`.
- The **Baseline** (`baseline.json`) freezes accepted Findings. The **Gate** is the pass/fail decision with a CI exit code.
- **Ratchet** (default) fails only on new Findings; **Absolute** mode uses fixed thresholds.
- `loam scan` runs a baseline-independent full audit; `loam health` reports complexity/health; `loam gate` is the CI check; `loam baseline --write` freezes the current state.

## Docs

- [How it works](https://getloam.dev/how-it-works/): the scan → Findings → Baseline → Gate pipeline.
- [Rules](https://getloam.dev/rules/): the live Rules and what each one detects.
- [Developer guide](https://getloam.dev/developer-guide/): install, commands, configuration and CI wiring.

## Reference

- [pub.dev package](https://pub.dev/packages/loam): install and version history.
- [Source on GitHub](https://github.com/silvio-l/loam): code, releases and issues.
- [Changelog](https://github.com/silvio-l/loam/blob/main/packages/loam_cli/CHANGELOG.md): release notes per version.
